perturbation.original_text: You agree to keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and not to use or disclose the Evaluation Material without the prior written consent of the Seller, except that, subject to the provisions hereof, you may disclose Evaluation Material to your Representatives to the extent that they need to know such Evaluation Material for purposes of the Evaluation, provided such Representatives are informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and the material terms of this Agreement and agree to be bound by the confidentiality provisions hereof (it being understood that, except in the case of financing sources, the foregoing shall not require any agreement in writing), and provided further that you shall be responsible for any unauthorized use or disclosure of Evaluation Material by your Representatives. You shall not make more copies of the Evaluation Material than are reasonably necessary to perform the Evaluation.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and not to use or disclose the Evaluation Material without the prior written consent of the Seller, except that, subject to the provisions hereof, you may disclose Evaluation Material to your Representatives to the extent that they need to know such Evaluation Material for purposes of the Evaluation, provided such Representatives are informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material and the material terms of this Agreement and agree to be bound by the confidentiality provisions hereof, and provided further that you shall be responsible for any unauthorized use or disclosure of Evaluation Material by your Representatives. You shall not make more copies of the Evaluation Material than are reasonably necessary to perform the Evaluation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing '(it being understood that, except in the case of financing sources, the foregoing shall not require any agreement in writing)', from Section 2, all Representatives are now required to agree in writing to the confidentiality provisions. This directly contradicts Section 8, which states in part 'your Representatives that are accounting firms, investment banks or similar organizations may, subject to the terms of this Agreement, retain copies of the Evaluation Material in accordance with policies and procedures implemented by such persons in order to comply with applicable law, regulation or professional standards (provided that such Representatives do not provide you access to any such retained Evaluation Material)'. This creates in-text contradiction because the previous section now requires them to sign an agreement in writing, but here it is implied that the Representative is able to retain copies with the terms of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If you decide that you do not wish to proceed with the Possible Transaction, you will promptly inform the Seller in writing. In that case, or at any time upon the request of the Seller for any reason, you will as promptly as practicable deliver to the Seller or destroy any and all Evaluation Material furnished to you or your Representatives and no copy thereof shall be retained, and you shall provide written confirmation of such destruction, provided, that (i) you or your Representatives may, subject to the terms of this Agreement, retain any electronic records or files containing Evaluation Material which have been created pursuant to automatic archiving and back-up procedures which cannot reasonably be deleted; provided that neither you nor your Representatives access such archives or back-up tapes for purposes of retrieving Evaluation Material, (ii) your outside legal counsel may, subject to the terms of this Agreement, retain in its files a copy of any Evaluation Material solely for use in connection with any disputes between the parties relating to the Evaluation Material or a possible negotiated transaction involving the parties and (iii) your Representatives that are accounting firms, investment banks or similar organizations may, subject to the terms of this Agreement, retain copies of the Evaluation Material in accordance with policies and procedures implemented by such persons in order to comply with applicable law, regulation or professional standards (provided that such Representatives do not provide you access to any such retained Evaluation Material). Notwithstanding the return or destruction of the Evaluation Material, you and your Representatives will continue to be bound by your obligations of confidentiality and nonuse and other obligations hereunder for a period of two years from the date first set forth above.
perturbation.justification: The change removes the exception for financing sources regarding the requirement for a written agreement to confidentiality. This directly contradicts the contradicted text in Section 8, which allows accounting firms, investment banks, and similar organizations to retain copies of Evaluation Material under certain policies without requiring a written agreement. By removing the exception, Section 2 now mandates a written agreement for all Representatives, including those mentioned in Section 8, creating a clear contradiction and imposing inconsistent obligations.

perturbation.original_text: You understand and agree that (a) the Seller (i) may terminate your access to Evaluation Material at any time, (ii) shall be free to conduct any process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine and (iii) shall be free at its sole discretion at any time to accept or reject any proposal relating to a Possible Transaction for any reason without notice to you or any third party, and (b) you shall have no claim against the Seller or its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: You understand and agree that (a) the Seller (i) may terminate your access to Evaluation Material at any time, (ii) shall be free to conduct any process for a Possible Transaction as it in its sole discretion shall determine, and (b) you shall have no claim against the Seller or its Representatives in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: Removing clause '(iii) shall be free at its sole discretion at any time to accept or reject any proposal relating to a Possible Transaction for any reason without notice to you or any third party' from Section 8 creates an ambiguity regarding the Seller's obligations. Section 15 states 'You hereby agree that unless and until a definitive agreement regarding the Possible Transaction is executed, neither the Seller nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this Agreement, except for matters specifically agreed to herein'. Without (iii), it could be argued that the Seller has some implied obligation to consider proposals fairly. This omission creates an inconsistency. Is the Seller free to reject proposals at any time (as implied by Section 15) or is there some implied obligation to consider them?
perturbation.contradicted_text: You hereby agree that unless and until a definitive agreement regarding the Possible Transaction is executed, neither the Seller nor you will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to such a transaction by virtue of this Agreement, except for matters specifically agreed to herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Seller can reject any proposal for any reason, without notice. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text states that neither party has any legal obligation unless a definitive agreement is executed, except for specifically agreed matters. The removal introduces a contradiction because without the explicit right to reject proposals for any reason, there's potential ambiguity about the Seller's obligations to consider proposals fairly. This creates a direct conflict with the intent of Section 15, as it could be argued that an implied obligation now exists, which is not 'specifically agreed' and therefore contradicts the clause stating no obligations exist until a definitive agreement is signed. The removal creates ambiguity where the original version had certainty, and conflicts with Section 15.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates, provided, that this Agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons; or (ii) the hiring of any such persons who initiate discussions with you regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates, provided, that this Agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause '(ii) the hiring of any such persons who initiate discussions with you regarding such employment without any direct or indirect solicitation by you' from Section 9, it creates a contradiction. The original version stated that you were not able to 'solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller'. By removing this clause, that employee isn't able to initiate a discussion for such employment if they aren't able to be hired, which contradicts with previous statement that states 'you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the Evaluation Material being furnished to you, you agree that for a period of two years from the date hereof, you will not, directly or indirectly, solicit for employment or employ any of the employees of the Seller while such employees remain employed by the Seller or its affiliates, provided, that this Agreement shall not prohibit (i) any general advertisement or general solicitation that is not specifically targeted at such persons.
perturbation.justification: The removal of clause (ii) from the original text in the changed text creates a contradiction. The original agreement explicitly allowed for the hiring of employees who initiated discussions without solicitation. By removing this exception, the changed text implies a complete ban on hiring employees of the Seller, even if they initiate the contact. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which outlines the initial agreement and its exceptions.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning subject matter hereof, and no modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall he binding upon either party hereto, unless approved in writing by each such party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning subject matter hereof, and no modification of this Agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall he binding upon either party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'unless approved in writing by each such party', this introduces an omission that creates an internal contradiction in Section 19. Without this clause, the process for modifying the agreement becomes undefined. This omission contradicts the intent of Section 11, which mentions waiver. If a waiver is not binding, how is a party waiving? How are they binding on this waiver, without having to do so by each party writing?
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that no failure or delay by either party in exercising any right, power or privilege hereunder shall operate as a waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial exercise thereof preclude any other or future exercise thereof or the exercise of any other right, power or privilege hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that modifications or waivers must be approved in writing by each party. The changed text removes this requirement, creating an omission. This omission directly contradicts Section 11, which addresses waivers. By removing the clause specifying the method of approval (in writing), it introduces ambiguity about how waivers are to be enacted and enforced. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists because the mechanism for valid waiver has been removed, creating an in-text contradiction.
